message: Only filter the package npm, not any package name contains 'npm'
removed_lines: -        INSTALLS=$(nvm deactivate > /dev/null && npm list -g --depth=0 | command tail -n +2 | command grep -o -e ' [^@]*' | command cut -c 2- | command grep -v npm | command xargs)
-        INSTALLS=$(nvm use "$VERSION" > /dev/null && npm list -g --depth=0 | command tail -n +2 | command grep -o -e ' [^@]*' | command cut -c 2- | command grep -v npm | command xargs)
added_lines: +        INSTALLS=$(nvm deactivate > /dev/null && npm list -g --depth=0 | command tail -n +2 | command grep -o -e ' [^@]*' | command cut -c 2- | command grep -vx npm | command xargs)
+        INSTALLS=$(nvm use "$VERSION" > /dev/null && npm list -g --depth=0 | command tail -n +2 | command grep -o -e ' [^@]*' | command cut -c 2- | command grep -vx npm | command xargs)

message:
tests: Fix grep syntax

In basic regular expressions | is not a special character that spearates
branches, so use an extended regular expression.
removed_lines: -  run_test "$data_file" | tee >($results_filter | ${0:A:h}/tap-colorizer.zsh) | grep -v '^not ok.*# TODO' | grep -q '^not ok\|^ok.*# TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))
added_lines: +  run_test "$data_file" | tee >($results_filter | ${0:A:h}/tap-colorizer.zsh) | grep -v '^not ok.*# TODO' | grep -Eq '^not ok|^ok.*# TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))

message: Fix bad ps syntax in ssh-agent plugin
